title: ageratina flourensifolia
intro: Ageratina flourensifolia, also known as Flourens' snakeroot or Mexican tea, is a plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is a perennial herb native to Mexico and Central America, traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's extracts have been found to possess health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving headaches and arthritis, and acting as an expectorant to alleviate respiratory issues. Ageratina flourensifolia is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
